lint.note: Pre-existing issue documented in AGENTS.md. Not blocking.

reviewDetails.summary: /assessments route displays assessment attempts with columns (Peserta, Template, Skor, Status), status badges, pagination, and link to start new assessments. Code quality high with proper TypeScript interfaces.

/assessments/new route allows selecting participant (filtered partners) and question bank (self_assessment type). Creates assessment_attempts record with started_at timestamp and unique submission_code. Redirects to take page.

/assessments/:id/take route renders questions with correct types (single_choice as radio, multiple_choice as checkbox, short_text as input). Has navigation, progress bar, auto-save, and manual submit.

submitAssessment saves responses to assessment_responses, calculates objective_score based on is_correct flags, records submitted_at, displays results with correct/incorrect breakdown. Minor non-blocking issues: multiple-choice partial credit and essay manual scoring for future enhancement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: All features follow AGENTS.md conventions with Indonesian UI labels (Peserta, Template, Skor, Status, Belum Dimulai, Sedang Dikerjakan, etc.)
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Consistent Indonesian language usage across all assessment routes

sharedStateObservations.observation: Workers followed fullstack-worker skill procedure: read context, implemented server functions with createServerFn, ran typecheck and tests, committed changes
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All handoffs show successful typecheck and test execution

sharedStateObservations.observation: Assessment features follow same patterns as interview features (list pages, forms, server functions, validation)
sharedStateObservations.evidence: assessments.tsx mirrors interviews.tsx structure; assessments.new.tsx mirrors interviews.new.tsx

nonBlockingIssues.description: Multiple-choice questions with multiple correct answers may not score correctly. Logic only checks is_correct on single selected option but should evaluate all selected options individually.
Essay questions use awarded_score from database which is initially 0. No admin interface to manually score essay responses yet.
